mnemonics.title: For Loop Structure
mnemonics.content: I C U - Initialization, Condition, Update

mnemonics.title: Break vs Continue
mnemonics.content: BREAK = Break out completely | CONTINUE = Carry on to next iteration

mnemonics.title: Loop Types
mnemonics.content: WHILE = Check FIRST, then do | DO-WHILE = Do FIRST, then check

acronyms.usage: Server-side technology where loops generate dynamic HTML
Protocol where loops iterate over request parameters
Loops in JSP generate repetitive HTML elements

visualTips.visualization: Imagine concentric circles - outer loop controls big circles, inner loop fills in details within each circle
Think of an emergency exit door - when reached, you exit the entire building (loop) immediately
Like skipping a step in a recipe but continuing with the rest of the dish
Like scanning items on a conveyor belt one by one without worrying about index numbers

analogies.analogy: Like a countdown timer - you know exactly how many times it will run: initialize, check condition, update, repeat
Like waiting at a red light - you keep checking (is it green?) until condition is met, may wait forever if never green
Like taking a driving test - you ALWAYS drive at least once before checking if you passed
Like using a master switch that turns off the entire building, not just the current room
Like a receptionist going through each name in the visitor's log one by one
